heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: No storm activity at the moment.  The previous storm occurred on 17 April 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 2nd since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 18 April 2026 and has lasted 14 days.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 0 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (213°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 18%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 2255 m) and light levels (up 9114 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1065 m), light levels (up 4020 lux), and outside temperature (down 2.2°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2250m lower than yesterday, 1065.2m lower than the past week, 36.9m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 2390m and a high of 8840m, averaging 2840m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.
cloud levels.summary: Expect mostly clear skies with high cloud.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; temperatures should feel nicely warm. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and winds will be practically absent. Chance of light rain, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and bright intervals likely. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
